abuse_cases.expected_behavior: pipeline halts; failing_command and failure_reason populated; stale_after_recompute false; HTTP 500
abuse_cases.actual_behavior: first non-zero exit halts loop; failedResult captured; subsequent steps not run; route returns 500

abuse_cases.case: graph generation succeeds but priority stale
abuse_cases.actual_behavior: after success path, route reloads priority via loadPriorityArtifact(); non-ok state sets stale_after_recompute and adds warning 'stale_after_recompute:priority_state=...:reason=...'
skipped step adds skip_reason to warnings; missing optional graph validation script does not fail the pipeline (advisory)

abuse_cases.expected_behavior: warning surfaces; pipeline does not crash
abuse_cases.actual_behavior: fs.existsSync gate adds explicit skipped step; warnings list 'missing_script:...'
loadPriorityArtifact() rerun catches invalid_schema / invalid_timestamp / future_timestamp; sets stale_after_recompute=true

abuse_cases.expected_behavior: recompute_unavailable_signal; do not pretend to run; surface exact local commands
abuse_cases.actual_behavior: process.env.VERCEL==='1' short-circuits to recompute_unavailable_signal with explicit commands in warnings

abuse_cases.case: stale artifact remains after recompute (silent overwrite)
abuse_cases.expected_behavior: do not overwrite last-known-good state; report failure plainly
abuse_cases.actual_behavior: route never writes artifacts itself; on script failure files on disk are unchanged from last successful run; failing_command exposes the exact command for operator investigation

outcome: All Phase 2 abuse cases addressed by the structured fail-closed response.
